title: Leakage-safe regime stacking, not probability calibration
summary: Prior work already explored regime ideas conceptually; the next testable extension is a strict two-level stack where specialists cover dry/wet, mandatory-stop-debt, and endgame regimes.
rationale: Prior work already explored regime ideas conceptually; the next testable extension is a strict two-level stack where specialists cover dry/wet, mandatory-stop-debt, and endgame regimes. Public calibration guidance also says calibration usually does not help rank metrics like AUC, so effort is better spent on rank-preserving OOF stacking than isotonic post-processing.
implementation_hint: Train 3-5 specialists on disjoint regimes or feature subsets, generate out-of-fold predictions with race-group CV, then fit a simple meta-learner on those OOF scores plus a few regime flags. Prefer sigmoid or plain linear/rank averaging if any post-transform is needed; avoid isotonic unless optimizing probability quality rather than AUC.
expected_effect: Can capture heterogeneous decision rules across slick/wet and early/late race without forcing one model to learn all regimes.
risk: Main failure mode is leakage from non-OOF base predictions or from fold schemes that mix rows from the same race. Over-partitioning regimes can also thin data.
